title: The Crypto Calculation Course
text:
About this course
This course is a companion to The Calculation Course. It is currently a stub — the syllabus is under development.
The course examines cryptocurrency and blockchain technology through the lens of heterodox political economy, asking: what do these technologies actually do, what do they claim to do, and how do they relate to the ecosocialist planning tradition developed in the Calculation Course?

title: The Crypto Calculation Course
text:
Planned scope
The course will cover:

Cryptographic and technical foundations — hash functions, public-key cryptography, consensus mechanisms, smart contracts
Bitcoin and the Austrian connection — the ideological roots of Bitcoin in Austrian economics; Hayek’s denationalization of money; what the calculation debate says about decentralized coordination
Ethereum and programmable money — smart contracts as planning primitives; DAOs as coordination mechanisms; the limits of on-chain governance
DeFi and financialization — automated market makers, liquidity pools, yield farming; how DeFi reproduces and amplifies capitalist financial dynamics
The ecosocialist critique — can blockchain technology serve planning rather than markets? Token systems, labor tokens, and the Cockshott-Dapprich tradition; what a socialist blockchain would actually need to do
Empirical analysis — on-chain data analysis with Python; measuring concentration, energy use, and financial flows

title: The Crypto Calculation Course
text:
Prerequisites
The Calculation Course through at least Week 8 (Planning and Allocation) and Controversy 2 (The Socialist Calculation Debate) and Controversy 6 (Monetary Theory of Production).
This page will be expanded into a full syllabus when the time comes.
